title: Rufaan Tigellinus
content:

{{Quote|Tigellinus' Star Rising in Imperial Court … Grand Admiral Rufaan Tigellinus is increasingly being seen as a force to be reckoned with in the Emperor's Court.|Alendar Jarvis, ''New Order Progressive''|<ref name="aj8">{{JournalCite|8|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>}}
'''Rufaan Tigellinus''' was a [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] and later a [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] until his [[Death/Legends|death]] in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Known for his [[Politics/Legends|political]] savvy and charisma, Tigellinus was widely followed in the media and was considered to be a key player in [[Imperial Court/Legends|Imperial Court]] politics. Already a respected [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|fleet admiral]], he was made a Grand Admiral in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and, from the helm of his [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], ''[[Avatar]]'', spent much time rooting out [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Tigellinus stepped up in the Imperial Court in a bid for further power, allying himself with the likes of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vilim Disra]] and [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] [[Alec Pradeux]], and becoming a member of the ancient, exclusive [[Order of the Canted Circle]]. When [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], an [[Chiss/Legends|alien]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], tried his hand at Imperial Court politics, the [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] Tigellinus befriended and later betrayed him, until Thrawn was unwelcome in the Empire.

Soon thereafter, Tigellinus brought his political clout to bear in order to have himself appointed as Grand Moff of the [[Imperial Center Oversector]] with the help of Vilim Disra, who became something of a mentor to Tigellinus. After [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine [[Battle of Endor/Legends#Demise of the Sith|perished]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Tigellinus became involved with the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]]. The Committee's leader, [[Bertroff Hissa]], a [[near-Human]] with whom Tigellinus had frequently butted heads, presented Tigellinus with a demeaning offer—a place on the Committee but not the leadership many had expected he would be awarded—hoping that he would refuse and give Hissa an excuse to execute him. Tigellinus prepared to accept the offer, but his close friend Disra advised against this; Tigellinus refused the appointment, and the Committee executed him for perceived disloyalty, while Disra laid claims to his assets and seat on the Committee.

==Biography==
===Grand Admiral===
{{Quote|More cutthroat than the Thalassians, more underhanded than the Black Hole Gang and more preposterous than Gir Kybo Ren Cha, the Poss'Nomin pirate Reginald Barkbone is the so-called Scourge of the Seven Sectors. What seven sectors this refers to is a mystery to me since I've chased his stolen Star Galleon ''Robber Baron'' and Incom corsairs from the Colonies to the Outer Rim and back.|Grand Admiral Rufaan Tigellinus, Star Destroyer ''Avatar'', Captain's Log, [[Carida system/Legends|Carida system]]|<ref name="underworld">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/89/indexp3.html|text=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues|int=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues}}</ref>}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Rufaan Tigellinus was a charismatic member of the [[Imperial Court/Legends|Imperial Court]], well known among the media,<ref name="who" /> and a respected strategist who served as a [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|fleet admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] for many years.<ref name="TEGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], during [[Fete Week|New Year Fete Week]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] created the rank of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]], appointing [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Tigellinus and eleven others to his "Circle of Twelve."<ref name="who" /> Tigellinus' reputation was such that his appointment met with little argument from those in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEGW" /> The Grand Admiral used his new power to further his position within the Imperial Court, gaining a large following.<ref name="who" />

Tigellinus commanded the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Avatar]]'' during the years preceding the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Around this time, he attempted to apprehend the [[Poss'Nomin]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Reginald Barkbone]], who had been plundering many [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] to the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]. Tigellinus and ''Avatar'' chased Barkbone through many [[Sector/Legends|sectors]], though the pirate's [[Star Galleon-class frigate|Star Galleon]], ''[[Robber Baron]]'', was able to escape each time. In his [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] log, Grand Admiral Tigellinus vowed to kill the pirate Barkbone; the log was later sent to [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] as part of an [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] report compiled by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Maximillian Seerdon]]. Around that same time, an [[Chiss/Legends|alien]] named [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] joined the Imperial Navy, earning the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. Tigellinus, a firm believer in [[Humanocentrism]], despised Thrawn, and began looking for ways to get rid of him.<ref name="underworld" />

Several months after the disastrous Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], Admiral Tigellinus started to become a major player in Imperial Court [[Politics/Legends|politics]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="aj9" /> He established an alliance with [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Alec Pradeux]], an [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] who was thought to be close to [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine, and forged a sizable faction in a short span of time. In late [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Tigellinus attempted to win support for his protégé, [[Gerald Weizel]], to become the [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]]. On [[Great ReSynchronization|36:9:4]], the media reported that Weizel had won the backing of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Jamson Caglio]]; this was an important event in Tigellinus' rise, as Caglio had been a firm supporter of Tigellinus' rival faction, that of [[Traeda]], and winning his support was seen as a large step toward the center of power. Around the same time, Admiral Tigellinus attempted to recruit Moff [[Vilim Disra]], a man with many ties to [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] Traeda but who had recently fallen out of favor with the Emperor, into his faction. The media kept a close watch on the situation, and [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]] [[Alendar Jarvis]] of the ''[[New Order Progressive]]'' published a report on Tigellinus' progress.<ref name="aj8" />

Admiral Tigellinus, along with Grand Moff Traeda and Moff [[Jaan]], officiated the festivities of that year's New Year Fete Week in the Emperor's absence, since the sovereign was away on Imperial business in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]].<ref name="aj8" /> Shortly thereafter, Thrawn, now an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]], entered Imperial politics. Tigellinus still harbored a deep hatred for [[Non-huMan|non-Humans]] and resented Thrawn's encroaching on his territory; he befriended the alien admiral, with the intention of later betraying him.<ref name="who" />

Around [[2 ABY/Legends|six months]] later, Tigellinus was inducted into the [[Order of the Canted Circle]], an elite, ancient, and highly exclusive social organization. The Order rarely took on more than eleven members at any one time, but Tigellinus was the thirteenth member added since [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]], so his invitation was a surprise to the media. It was a testament to Tigellinus' rising stature that he was selected to join ahead of swaths of prospective members, each revered leaders of the Imperial government, most of whom had been waiting for an invitation for several years. Tigellinus wore the traditional black velvet robe of the Order during his public induction ceremony at the [[Skydome Botanical Gardens/Legends|Skydome Botanical Gardens]]; later that evening, a private ceremony was held in an undisclosed location, and Tigellinus became a fully-fledged member of what was considered one of the most prestigious social clubs in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="aj9" />

Tigellinus' joining the Order was somewhat overshadowed by the induction of Thrawn, who had returned to Coruscant after an extended trip to the Outer Rim, several months later. Rumors abounded that the Emperor had personally requested that the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] admiral be invited, but his invitation was still considered remarkable; he was the fourteenth member and he was a non-Human. Tigellinus was outraged and attempted to use his influence to halt Thrawn's joining, to no avail. Though he did the best he could to conceal his actions in an attempt to maintain his charade with Thrawn, the media assumed it had been Tigellinus leading the opposition.<ref name="aj12">{{JournalCite|12|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>

===Grand Moff===
[[File:Vaderrotj.jpg|left|thumb|Darth Vader, the Sith Lord Tigellinus met with.]]
{{Quote|Grand Admiral Takel was executed by Trioculus, while the Central Committee of Grand Moffs did away with Grand Admiral Tigellinus for perceived disloyalty.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]|<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>}}
Around the same time, Grand Admiral Tigellinus' bid for further power was given a tremendous boost when the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]],<ref name="kenner">{{KennerCite|set=Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (toy line)|pack=Darth Vader vs. Prince Xizor|link=soteKxizor-vader.asp}}</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] - one of his chief rivals on Coruscant - left the capital to lead [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] in the Outer Rim, for what was expected by the media to be a considerable amount of time. Tigellinus met with Vader just days later as part of his duties, during the unveiling of the latter's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], the first of its kind, near [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]]. Tigellinus, Vader, Thrawn, and several others flanked Palpatine during the unveiling ceremony.<ref name="aj12" />

Tigellinus and Thrawn were among several high profile absentees from the [[Shaldania Parade]] at the start of Coruscant's New Year Fete Week on 38:F1:1. Though their absence was officially attributed to them being on assignments elsewhere in the Empire, [[Galaxy News Service]] reported that both admirals' factions in the Imperial Court were maneuvering for more powerful positions.<ref name="aj13">{{JournalCite|13|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref> Tigellinus soon enacted his plan to ensure that Thrawn was out of favor in Palpatine's Court; he betrayed the alien admiral, who ultimately became not only unwelcome on Coruscant, but within the entire Empire. Thrawn was exiled to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], and Tigellinus was believed to have achieved a great victory. In actual fact, the Chiss [[Commander/Legends|commander's]] falling out of favor was orchestrated by the Emperor for his own clandestine means.<ref name="who" /> Tigellinus also took a dislike to the vicious tendencies of [[Delak Krennel]], the violent–but–effective captain of the Star Destroyer [[Reckoning (Imperial II-class)|''Reckoning'']], and in order to both rid himself of Krennel and to vex Thrawn, Tigellinus had Krennel expelled to the Unknown Regions to serve under the Chiss.<ref name="Despoilers of an Empire">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-1|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

Soon thereafter, Tigellinus decided that he was best suited to governing regions rather than protecting them, and used his considerable political influences to bring about an appointment to Grand Moff. He had many allies, though he was also at odds with many of his fellow Grand Moffs, including [[Bertroff Hissa]], a [[near-Human]] Moff. Tigellinus was something of a rival with Vilim Disra, whom he had once courted, but Disra's assistance to the Grand Admiral-cum-Grand Moff was immeasurable; with Disra's aid, Tigellinus took control of the [[Imperial Center Oversector]], considered to be among the most important sectors in the galaxy.<ref name="who" />

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="3ABY">"[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]" in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 14|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 14]] provides a date of 38:5:11. {{YearMathGRS}}</ref> on 38:5:11, the Emperor assigned Tigellinus to head an investigation into the affairs of one [[Jerrod Maclain]], the governor of [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal]], who had been accused of corruption and "behavior unsuitable for an Imperial diplomat." Tigellinus' appointment was to the great surprise of Imperial Court observers, who considered him too important and influential to be relegated to such rudimentary investigative duties, though Alec Pradeux, his longtime ally, informed the media that Palpatine's choosing such a prominent individual merely highlighted the gravity of the situation. Tigellinus addressed the media about the impending investigation, informing them of his suspicions that those who had sold Maclain out were embroiled in a plot to steal his power. He also told the media that Maclain was a loyal servant of the Empire, and that Tigellinus would carry out as thorough an inquest as he could muster.<ref name="aj14">{{JournalCite|14|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>

After Palpatine and his top [[Sith apprentice/Legends|lieutenant]], Vader, [[Battle of Endor/Legends#Demise of the Sith|perished]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Empire descended into chaos, and many sought to claim the Emperor's former power.<ref name="nec" /> Tigellinus continued to defend his territories while waiting to see who came to power as Palpatine's successor.<ref name="TEGW" /> Eventually, [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] emerged as the new leader of the Empire, though many others lusted for power and opposed her.<ref name="nec" /> A group of Moffs, led by Grand Moffs Bertroff Hissa, [[Dunhausen]], [[Thistleborn]] and [[Muzzer]], formed the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]], which openly opposed Isard and [[Trioculus' Empire|claimed the throne]] for their ally, [[Trioculus]].<ref name="godv">''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''</ref> Because of his previous power and influence, Tigellinus was seen by many as a natural leader of the Committee, though Hissa, Tigellinus' enemy from his early days as a Grand Moff, presented him with a demeaning offer: he could become a member of the Committee, but nothing more. Hissa engineered events so that, should Tigellinus refuse, he would be executed. On the advice of his trusted colleague Disra, Tigellinus declined the offer,<ref name="who" /> and Hissa and his allies killed him.<ref name="nec" /> Disra's machinations allowed him to absorb Tigellinus' assets and take his place on the Committee.<ref name="who" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|One way or another, I vow to wipe Reginald Barkbone from the fabric of space forever … then I'll see to that cobalt-colored fellow Captain Thrawn.|Tigellinus' Log|<ref name="underworld" />}}
Rufaan Tigellinus was a highly driven, highly motivated man, with abundant charisma and political savvy<ref name="who" /> who was known for his tactical abilities.<ref name="TEGW" /> His ambition saw him rise extremely high in Imperial politics, becoming one of the Emperor's most trusted minions;<ref name="who" /> however, others, such as Darth Vader, thought little of the Grand Admirals.<ref name="negtc">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> Tigellinus was adept at making allies who would benefit his rise to power, and he demonstrated this numerous times during his career—Alec Pradeux and Vilim Disra, both political powerhouses, were two friends who greatly helped Tigellinus establish himself in the Imperial Court.<ref name="aj8" /><ref name="who" /> He used unscrupulous means to achieve his goals, befriending Thrawn with the intention of betraying him, merely because the admiral was not Human. Despite this, Tigellinus was easily manipulated by Disra; his trust in his friend allowed Disra to orchestrate his downfall.<ref name="who" /> He was also very persistent, chasing the pirate Reginald Barkbone through many systems and refusing to give up,<ref name="underworld" /> and he told the media he would conduct a proper and thorough investigation into the events surrounding Governor Maclain.<ref name="aj14" />

Tigellinus was very Humanocentric and he despised non-Humans<ref name="aj12" /> and near-Humans.<ref name="who" /> When he learned of Thrawn, then a captain in the Imperial Navy, he vowed to deal with the Chiss and ensure that his Imperial career was short-lived.<ref name="underworld" /> Later, he betrayed Thrawn,<ref name="who" /> and his animosity toward the alien was not a closely guarded secret.<ref name="aj12" /> Tigellinus also clashed with a near-Human, Bertroff Hissa, many times while serving as a Grand Moff.<ref name="who" /> The open disgust Tigellinus showed towards Hissa was not forgotten by the [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] Grand Moff,<ref name="BT:AHofE">{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-1|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> who later engineered Tigellinus' demise.<ref name="who" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Tigellinus was beloved: intelligent, highly capable, a smooth talker, and young … the idealized Imperial. With that kind of pull, it certainly might've been possible for him to be recognized as both a Grand Admiral and Grand Moff, at least informally. The Committee of Grand Moffs certainly saw it that way when they offered Tigellinus to join them.|Abel G. Peña|<ref name="sw.com">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=169992&start=135#9435937|thread=The other Imperial Grand Admirals…|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad]]|date=2005-01-30|time=12:49 PM}}</ref>}}
Rufaan Tigellinus was created by [[Paul Sudlow]] for ''[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]'', which was a recurring series of articles that appeared in the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''; he first appeared in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|issue eight]], published in [[November]] [[1995]],<ref name="aj8" /> and made frequent appearances until [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 14|issue fourteen]],<ref name="aj9" /><ref name="aj12" /><ref name="aj14" /> some of which were written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]].<ref name="aj14" /> Tigellinus was the third named Grand Admiral, a rank first appearing in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''Thrawn Trilogy'']]; the other two were Thrawn, first appearing in ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', and [[Josef Grunger]] from ''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''.<ref name="godv" /><ref name="htte">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref>

Tigellinus went unreferenced for many years; he was not mentioned in [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], released in [[2000]], which dealt with the ultimate fate of many of the Grand Admirals.<ref name="tec">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> Wallace made mention to the character in [[2002]]'s ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'',<ref name="negtc" /> and later, he and [[Abel G. Peña]] expanded Tigellinus' history and backstory in the character's bio in ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', published in [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]].<ref name="who" /> ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'' was originally slated to appear under the title of ''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]'' in an edition of ''[[Star Wars Gamer]] Magazine'', but the magazine was canceled and the article was moved to ''Star Wars Insider'', and much of the original content was cut.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref><ref name="document">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref> Some of this cut content, such as Tigellinus' Star Destroyer, ''Avatar'', has since been mentioned by other sources and made [[canon]];<ref name="underworld" /><ref name="document" /> the remainder of it is summarized in the next section below. In addition to cut content regarding his life, the original draft also included [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] statistics, including Tigellinus' equipment: a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], his [[Code cylinder/Legends|code cylinder]], a pendant of the Order of the Canted Circle, and an ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer, named ''Avatar''.<ref name="document" /> Daniel Wallace later posted the equipment list for each of the Grand Admirals on his [[StarWars.com]] blog.<ref name="blog" /> Tigellinus has since been mentioned in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', ''[[Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues]]'' and ''[[Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire]]''.<ref name="underworld" /><ref name="nec" /><ref name="BT:AHofE" />

In ''Galaxywide NewsNets'' from ''Star Wars Adventure Journal 14'', Tigellinus is inexplicably referred to as a "Grand Moff,"<ref name="aj14" /> despite all previous ''NewsNets'' articles giving him the title of "Grand Admiral"—a demotion.<ref name="aj9" /><ref name="aj8" /><ref name="aj12" /> Peña and Wallace established that Tigellinus decided to become a Grand Moff around the time of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']];<ref name="who" /> however, there has been some confusion among fans as to whether Tigellinus retained his rank or if a new Grand Admiral was appointed, as Palpatine wished for there to be twelve Grand Admirals at any one time. Peña stated on the StarWars.com message boards that, in his view, it was possible for Tigellinus to hold both positions, at least informally, because of his political influence.<ref name="sw.com" /> In [[2008]], Tigellinus received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], [[Pablo Hidalgo]], Daniel Wallace and others.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|III|247|Tigellinus, Grand Admiral Rufaan}}</ref>

{{Cutstart}}
===Cut content===
Tigellinus emerged on the Imperial Court politics scene at a rapid pace, building up a large following; he drew people into his influence rapidly, and those who opposed him quickly found their careers in tatters. Disra, Tigellinus' mentor, advised him to "know your enemies as intimately as friends," so when Thrawn, who Tigellinus considered an alien freak, became involved in Imperial politics, Tigellinus did just that; he helped Thrawn gain admission to the Order of the Canted Circle, wherein he consistently undermined the alien's influence and turned the Order against him. Thrawn was possessed of considerable intellect, which few were able to match; Tigellinus managed to do that and more, orchestrating—he believed—Thrawn's banishment. Tigellinus gave Thrawn credit, claiming he played the game well, but was very confident in his own intellect.<ref name="document" />

Tigellinus thought little of his fellow Grand Admirals; he saw some as wholly incompetent, others as talented military strategists possessing no tact or guile off the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], and was quick to locate the character flaws of many of his peers. Tigellinus relished a challenge, and after Thrawn's exile he saw none in the ranks of the Grand Admirals. When he looked at the Grand Moffs, however, he saw many worthy adversaries—[[Ardus Kaine]], [[Kentor Sarne]] and [[Nigel Nivers|Nivers]]—so he became a Grand Moff himself.<ref name="document" />
{{Cutend}}
